name: Arunachal Pradesh
stopoverNote: Arunachal is a permit-first, road-first trip: Indian citizens must obtain a Tourist eILP before entry (official eILP site says ₹300 up to 3 days, ₹500 beyond 3 and up to 14 days; longer stays use provisional eILP), while foreign nationals need PAP/RAP via FRRO/authorized channels. Use Guwahati, Tezpur or Hollongi/Itanagar as gateways, but treat Tezpur/Bhalukpong→Dirang→Tawang as a two-day mountain approach over Sela Pass; snow, rain, landslides, army controls and altitude can delay plans.

diet.vegetarian: Vegetarian food is workable in Bomdila, Dirang, Tawang and Ziro if requested early: veg thukpa, momos, dal-rice, noodles, khura pancakes, butter tea and simple potato/greens are common. Remote Monpa and tribal homestays may default to pork, yak, chicken, fish or fermented meat, so confirm meals before booking and carry snacks for long drives.
diet.vegan: Vegan travel needs advance notice because butter tea, ghee, curd, paneer and meat stock appear in fixed homestay meals, especially around Tawang, Sangti and Ziro villages. Ask for oil-only dal, rice, steamed vegetables, veg momos without butter, black tea and no egg/meat stock; options are easier in larger towns than on Sela/Bum La road days.
phrases: Maas aur anda ke bina shakahari khana chahiye = I need vegetarian food without meat or egg
Dudh, ghee, makkhan ya paneer mat daliye = please do not add milk, ghee, butter or paneer
Nga veg khana chahiye, meat stock nahi = I need veg food, no meat stock

links.label: UNESCO Tentative List — Apatani cultural landscape

cities.name: Bomdila
cities.notes: West Kameng transit ridge with monasteries, craft centres and Himalayan viewpoints; the right place to break the long Assam-to-Tawang climb.

cities.name: Dirang
cities.notes: A lower-altitude Monpa valley base for Sangti Valley, hot springs, orchards and a safer acclimatization night before Sela Pass.

cities.name: Tawang
cities.notes: High Himalayan Monpa town anchored by Tawang Monastery, Sela Pass, war memorials, glacier lakes and altitude-sensitive Bum La excursions.

cities.name: Ziro Valley
cities.notes: Apatani cultural landscape and music-festival valley with paddy-fish farms, pine hills, village walks and a UNESCO tentative-list story.

itinerary.sim: Jio and Airtel work in the larger towns, while BSNL often survives in remote valleys; expect dead zones around Sela Pass, Bum La, Sangti side roads and long forested stretches. Download maps, carry hotel/driver numbers offline and keep paper/eILP copies because checkposts may not have reliable data.

Bomdila.name: Bomdila
Bomdila.note: Use Bomdila as a genuine overnight break, not a photo stop: Guwahati to Bomdila can take most of a day, and pushing onward toward Tawang the same evening is unsafe and exhausting.

days.theme: Permit check, West Kameng climb & Bomdila gompas
days.note: Enter Arunachal only after your eILP is approved and printed/saved offline; the Tezpur/Bhalukpong checkpost may ask for ID and permit. Guwahati→Bomdila is often 8–10 hours and Tezpur→Bomdila about 5–6 hours, so keep sightseeing light.

activities.name: Bhalukpong/Arunachal entry checkpost and eILP verification
activities.cost: Tourist eILP ₹300 up to 3 days; ₹500 for 4–14 days
activities.tip: Carry the same photo ID used online, permit print/PDF and multiple copies; children below 14 travel with an adult's paperwork per eILP guidance.

activities.name: Bomdila Upper Gompa and prayer hall
activities.tip: Reach before dusk; respect monastery photography restrictions.

activities.name: Bomdila viewpoint and RR Hill sunset
activities.tip: Views of Kangto/Gorichen depend on clear post-monsoon or spring mornings.

activities.name: Craft centre and local market browse

days.theme: Optional Bomdila buffer and orchid detour
days.note: Use this only if the Assam approach was delayed or you want a slower West Kameng acclimatization day before Dirang and Sela Pass.

activities.name: Lower and Middle Gompa circuit
Apple orchard or craft shopping pause
Tipi Orchid Centre detour if approaching from Bhalukpong
activities.tip: Only do this if it fits the road direction; do not backtrack from Bomdila on a Tawang-bound day.

Dirang.name: Dirang
Dirang.note: Two days lets you avoid a rushed Sela crossing: one gentle valley day after Bomdila, then one honest transfer day to Tawang over the 13,700-ft pass.

days.theme: Dirang Dzong, hot springs & Sangti Valley
days.note: Dirang sits far lower than Tawang, making it the most comfortable acclimatization base before Sela Pass. Keep the day valley-focused and avoid late-night driving on narrow roads.

activities.name: Dirang Dzong village walk
activities.tip: Walk respectfully through lived-in lanes; ask before photographing residents.

activities.name: Dirang hot water spring
activities.tip: Carry sandals and a towel; facilities are basic.

activities.name: Sangti Valley river and village loop
activities.tip: Black-necked crane sightings are seasonal and never guaranteed; keep the valley quiet.

activities.name: National Yak Research Centre viewpoint stop

days.theme: Dirang to Tawang via Sela Pass and Jaswant Garh
days.note: This is a transfer day across Sela Pass at roughly 13,700 ft. Start early, layer up, avoid alcohol the previous night, walk slowly at the pass and expect snow, fog, army convoys or roadwork to stretch a 5–6 hour drive.

activities.name: Sela Pass and Sela Lake halt
activities.tip: Do not linger if anyone has headache, nausea or breathlessness; descend toward Tawang.

activities.name: Jaswant Garh War Memorial
activities.tip: A practical warm tea and restroom stop between Sela and Tawang.

activities.name: Nuranang/Jang Falls detour if roads are clear
activities.tip: Skip in fog, heavy rain or if the Sela crossing already ran late.

activities.name: Tawang check-in and rest
activities.tip: Hydrate and keep the first Tawang evening low-effort for altitude.

Tawang.name: Tawang
Tawang.note: Tawang needs time: one monastery/culture day, one lake-and-war-memorial day, one high-altitude Bum La buffer, and one flexible day because Sela/Bum La roads are weather- and army-control dependent.

days.theme: Tawang Monastery, Ani Gompa & acclimatized town day
days.note: Keep the first full day in and around town. Tawang is around 10,000 ft, so this culture day helps before attempting Bum La or high lakes.

activities.name: Tawang Monastery
activities.tip: Go early for prayer ambience; follow no-photo rules inside halls and libraries.

activities.name: Tawang War Memorial
activities.tip: Evening light-and-sound schedules vary; verify locally before planning dinner around it.

activities.name: Buddha Park viewpoint
Old market and Monpa craft browse

meals: Khura with butter tea if you take dairy

days.theme: Pankang Teng Tso, Urgelling & quiet monastery circuit
days.note: This stays closer to town than Bum La and works as a weather-flex day. Lakes can be icy in winter and muddy in monsoon; carry warm layers year-round.

activities.name: Pankang Teng Tso (PT Tso Lake)
activities.tip: Road access depends on weather; skip if your driver advises against it.

activities.name: Urgelling Monastery
Khinmey or Gyangong Ani Gompa
activities.tip: Confirm access with locals because smaller gompas may close without notice.

activities.name: Local café and altitude rest block

days.theme: Bum La, Sangetsar Lake & high-altitude border route
days.note: Bum La is a serious high-altitude excursion near 15,000 ft and requires a local vehicle plus army/local permit paperwork; foreigners are generally not allowed. Leave before sunrise, carry ID/eILP, skip if unwell, and accept same-day cancellation for snow or army restrictions.

activities.name: Bum La Pass permit and army checkpost route
activities.tip: Only local Tawang vehicles normally run this road; arrange paperwork a day earlier through your hotel/driver.

activities.name: Sangetsar (Madhuri) Lake
activities.cost: Included in local vehicle loop / varies
activities.tip: Walk slowly and avoid running for photos at altitude.

activities.name: Nagula lake-view stops
activities.cost: Included in local vehicle loop / varies

activities.name: Early return and warm rest in Tawang

days.theme: Buffer day for Sela/Bum La delays or Nuranang Falls
days.note: Do not plan a tight flight or Ziro transfer immediately after a must-do Bum La day. Use this buffer for weather slippage, monastery revisits, laundry and rest before the long exit drive.

activities.name: Reserve day for cancelled Bum La or lake route
activities.tip: This is the difference between a feasible Arunachal plan and a paper itinerary.

activities.name: Nuranang Falls if missed on arrival
Tawang market, monastery revisit or rest

days.note: Add this only for a slower trip or festival timing such as Losar/Torgya; it is not needed if roads are clear and you are exiting after four Tawang nights.

activities.name: Monpa village or craft visit with local guide
Second Tawang Monastery morning prayers
Café, rest and permit/route reconfirmation

Ziro Valley.name: Ziro Valley
Ziro Valley.note: Ziro is a separate central-Arunachal leg, best reached from Naharlagun/Itanagar or after breaking the Tawang exit in Assam; do not attempt Tawang-to-Ziro as a same-day tourist transfer.

days.theme: Apatani villages, paddy-fish farms & Old Ziro
days.note: If coming from Tawang, arrive only after an overnight road break via Tezpur/Itanagar/Naharlagun. Ziro's value is slow cultural context, so hire a local guide and avoid treating villages like photo sets.

activities.name: Hong/Hari Apatani village walk with local guide
activities.tip: Ask permission before entering homes or photographing people.

activities.name: Tarin Fish Farm and paddy-cum-pisciculture fields
activities.tip: This is the living landscape behind the UNESCO tentative-list story.

activities.name: Pine Grove and Old Ziro viewpoints
Local market and Apatani food tasting

meals: Black tea; avoid apong if you do not drink

days.theme: Dolo Mando hike, Meghna Cave Temple & festival valley
days.note: March–May and October–November have the best hiking weather; September can be rewarding for Ziro Festival but roads, stays and permits must be booked early and rain remains possible.

activities.name: Dolo Mando viewpoint hike
activities.tip: Trails are slippery after rain; wear shoes with grip.

activities.name: Meghna Cave Temple
Ziro Festival of Music grounds or artisan visit
activities.tip: Festival dates/tickets change annually; September is not the driest month, so pack rain gear.

activities.name: Departure toward Naharlagun/Hollongi/Itanagar

days.theme: Optional Talley Valley or slow Apatani immersion
days.note: Add a third day only if you want a deeper nature/culture stay or festival recovery; Talley Valley access and guide logistics should be confirmed locally.

activities.name: Talley Valley nature excursion with local guide
Midey pine and bamboo walk
Homestay cooking conversation and rest
activities.tip: Confirm vegetarian/vegan ingredients before the homestay shops for dinner.

connections.method: Private/shared Sumo on the West Kameng mountain road
connections.cost: ₹2,500–₹4,000 private taxi segment; shared Sumo varies

connections.method: Private/local taxi via Sela Pass, Jaswant Garh and Jang
Two-day road exit via Bomdila/Tezpur or Itanagar/Naharlagun, then Ziro taxi/shared Sumo
connections.cost: Multi-day vehicle cost varies widely; do not price as a same-day transfer

extras: ILP is not optional: Indian citizens need eILP before entering Arunachal; the official tourist form shows ₹300 up to 3 days and ₹500 for 4–14 days, with provisional eILP used for longer visits.
Foreign nationals need PAP/RAP, not ILP; the eILP site points foreigners to FRRO/e-services, and PAP conditions can restrict solo/route travel, so verify before booking remote areas.
Sela Pass is high enough for altitude symptoms and winter snow closures; keep the Bomdila/Dirang overnight breaks and never plan Guwahati/Tezpur to Tawang as one comfortable sightseeing day.
Bum La and high lakes depend on army/local permit controls and weather; arrange through a Tawang hotel/driver at least a day ahead and keep a buffer day.
Monsoon months bring landslides and slow roads across West Kameng and Lower Subansiri; Oct–Nov and Mar–May are the most reliable windows for this combined Tawang–Ziro route.
